narrative: Middle East escalation and Strait of Hormuz attacks revive USD demand, while a jump in NY Fed consumer inflation expectations to the highest since 2023 supports a hawkish Fed outlook. The dollar firms as equities slip and crude surges — gold lower -0.25%; equities -0.30%; DXY +0.16%; VIX 15.8. USD/JPY holds at 161.94, supported by a +275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage, though a strong options put skew of -1.52 on 25d RR (Saxo, 1M, indicative mid) signals elevated downside hedging demand. Watch the next RBNZ meeting as the next key policy catalyst.
